mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which amplifies corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of co-expansion periods, indicating no incremental predictive value from the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 225.0, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, causing rapid equity repricing and negative short-term S&P 500 returns as discount rates rise and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn positive in greater than 50% of high-inflation months, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 240.0, invalidating the tightening regime assumption.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 45% during industrial expansion phases, indicating a broken supply-earnings transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months with payroll gains above 200,000, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in real GDP and retail sales indicates a synchronized demand shock that forces corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint contraction fails to predict negative returns, with positive forward returns occurring in greater than 50% of instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery environments, simultaneous rebounds in industrial production and housing starts signal rapid supply chain normalization and infrastructure investment, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain flat or negative in greater than 60% of recovery periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below baseline during non-recovery regimes, invalidating the regime-specific assumption.
